notes: Third band take (Peggy-1.m4a, ~66kbps AAC, 356.2s). Fastest and hottest mix of the three takes; vocal sits noticeably lower relative to the band (rung-3 mel head AUC drops to ~0.62 here vs ~0.96 on take 2 -- the current hard test case). Confirmed by the band: first sung line at 0:18, guitar solo starts 2:20 (vocals end a few beats before), 'if ever I return' re-entry at 3:45, and Corby's shouted 'damn' at ~3:15 during the solo (mel raw curve shows vocal-like bursts at 3:12 and 3:19-3:21). The _damn section excludes that window from the instrumental class. End-of-vocals (~5:40) inferred, not confirmed. Block sections named verse_1 / verse_5 so probe anchor logic works: only their START times are confirmed; each block spans four verses.

sections.notes: Someone talking right at the top of the recording
Verses 1-4 block; start confirmed by band (0:18), end = a few beats before the 2:20 solo
Corby's 'damn' (~3:15 per band, vocal bursts at 3:12 / 3:19-3:21 in the mel curve). Excluded so the shout never trains as 'instrumental'.
Verses 5-8 block; start confirmed by band (3:45), end inferred (~5:40)
